# Assia Kasdi Named 100 Women to Watch in Climate HardTech

![View organization page for Milvus Advanced]()

2,831 followers

Proud to have our Founder and CEO Assia Kasdi named among 100 Women to Watch in Climate HardTech, alongside so many brilliant founders building Europe's next wave of climate infrastructure.
At [Milvus Advanced](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/milvus-advanced?trk=public_post-text), we are replacing rare and critical metals like iridium, platinum and indium with earth-abundant nanoalloys, reducing dependence on fragile supply chains and accelerating the clean-tech transition. This list is a great reminder of what is being built across Europe right now.
[https://lnkd.in/evnZnynP](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FevnZnynP&urlhash=PhBL&trk=public_post-text)
🌍 [#IWD2026](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fiwd2026&trk=public_post-text) [#WomenInClimate](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fwomeninclimate&trk=public_post-text) [#WomenInScience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fwomeninscience&trk=public_post-text) [#Climatetech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fclimatetech&trk=public_post-text) [#MilvusAdvanced](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmilvusadvanced&trk=public_post-text) [#CriticalMaterials](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcriticalmaterials&trk=public_post-text) [#AdvancedManufacturing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fadvancedmanufacturing&trk=public_post-text) [#CleanEnergy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcleanenergy&trk=public_post-text)

100 one's to watch in Climate HardTech.
Despite capital still flowing disproportionately elsewhere, women across Europe are not waiting for permission to build.
As they quietly raise rounds, secure grants, commission factories, test full-scale systems and scale industrial technologies to define the next decade.
From AI-powered peptide discovery and precision fermentation to battery recycling, carbon capture and biodiversity intelligence, the next wave of climate infrastructure is being built with grit and engineering at its core.
What’s striking is not just the technologies themselves, but who is building them.
Enter: A powerful cohort of women Founders and CEOs redefining what climate leadership looks like as they rewire supply chains, restore nature at scale, transform food systems, decarbonise heavy industry and reinvent industrial chemistry.
Ahead of International Women’s Day, we highlighting 100 women behind Europe’s industrial climate renaissance to showcase the builders turning Europe’s climate ambition into reality.
[Caroline Thaler](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/caroline-thaler-35170599?trk=public_post_reshare-text), [Dali Rashid,](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/dali-rashid-phd?trk=public_post_reshare-text) Assia 100 one's to watch in Climate HardTech.
Despite capital still flowing disproportionately elsewhere, women across Europe are not waiting for permission to build.
As they quietly raise rounds, secure grants, commission factories, test full-scale systems and scale industrial technologies to define the next decade.
From AI-powered peptide discovery and precision fermentation to battery recycling, carbon capture and biodiversity intelligence, the next wave of climate infrastructure is being built with grit and engineering at its core.
What’s striking is not just the technologies themselves, but who is building them.
Enter: A powerful cohort of women Founders and CEOs redefining what climate leadership looks like as they rewire supply chains, restore nature at scale, transform food systems, decarbonise heavy industry and reinvent industrial chemistry.
Ahead of International Women’s Day, we highlighting 100 women behind Europe’s industrial climate renaissance to showcase the builders turning Europe’s climate ambition into reality.

Ahead of International Women’s Day, we are very proud to see our Founder and CEO, [Agnes Czako](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/agnes-czako-9476453b?trk=public_post-text), recognised in the '100 Ones to Watch in Climate Hard Tech'.
Across Europe, a new generation of founders and engineers is building the infrastructure behind the climate transition. Agnes is helping drive that transformation in housing, leading the development of retrofit technologies designed to make homes healthier, more energy efficient and easier to upgrade at scale.
Congratulations to all the women recognised who are helping shape Europe’s climate future. 🌍

At Envirometrics, women are an essential part of the team driving Greece's green transition. Here's what that looks like in practice:
📐 Nearly all hold Master's degrees in environmental science, climate change, engineering, and sustainability.
📅 Some have dedicated 8, 10, 11, even 17 years to this mission (long before "ESG" became a buzzword).
🔬 They lead and work at our laboratory, our carbon footprint projects, our GHG verification, and our business development.
👩💼 They sit on our BoD.
👩👧 Several are also mothers, proving every day that ambition and family are not in conflict when the environment is right.
From calculating carbon footprints to advising on sustainable finance and climate strategy, the women of Envirometrics are not behind the scenes. They are the science.
On this IWD, to every woman on our team: thank you for your expertise, your dedication, and your drive to make the planet a measurably better place.
Happy International Women's Day!

Call for Applications:
Standard Chartered Foundation Women in Tech Accelerator 2026 (Up to $10,000 Funding
Women founders and early-stage tech innovators are invited to apply for the Standard Chartered Foundation Women in Tech Accelerator 2026, a programme that supports female-led tech ventures with mentorship, business development support, community connections, and up to $10,000 in non-equity funding to help scale solutions that address real market needs.
This initiative is part of the ’s commitment to empowering women in technology and bridging gender gaps in the digital economy.
What the Women in Tech Accelerator Offers
Selected participants will benefit from:
💡 Funding Support
Up to $10,000 in non-equity funding to help grow your tech venture, strengthen your product, or expand your reach.
🧠 Accelerator Support
Practical training and workshops covering:
Product and business model refinement
Go-to-market strategy
Financial planning
Tech scaling and operations
🤝 Mentorship and Coaching
Access to industry mentors, experienced founders, and technical advisors to guide growth and execution.
🌍 Community & Network
Connection to a pan-African network of women tech founders and ecosystem partners.
📈 Exposure and Visibility
Opportunities to showcase your solution to potential partners, investors, and stakeholders.
Who Should Apply
This accelerator is designed for:
Women founders or co-founders of tech-enabled startups
Entrepreneurs building innovative tech solutions with market potential
Early-stage companies with a working product, pilot, or clear pathway to traction
Solutions that address real world problems across sectors like FinTech, HealthTech, EdTech, AgriTech, AI, digital services and more
Founders from across Africa are encouraged to apply. The programme is especially suited for women ready to scale operations and strengthen their market presence.
Application Timeline
📅 Applications Open: 2 March 2026
📅 Deadline: 27 March 2026
Make sure to complete and submit your application before the deadline.
Why This Opportunity Matters
Despite rapid growth in Africa’s digital economy, women founders often face significant barriers including limited access to capital, mentorship, and growth networks. The Standard Chartered Foundation Women in Tech Accelerator addresses these gaps by providing:
Strategic support tailored for women in tech
Funding that does not dilute ownership
Mentorship to accelerate business readiness
Community infrastructure to sustain growth
For women building technology solutions that create social and economic value, this programme could provide a key boost toward scalability and visibility.
How to Apply
Submit your application via the official programme portal:
👉 [https://lnkd.in/drr7FWpe](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fdrr7FWpe&urlhash=SDpw&trk=public_post-text)
Applicants should review eligibility criteria and ensure all required information is provided accurately

Who is a woman who’s made an impact on your career? [#IWD26](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fiwd26&trk=public_post-text) Magda Machulska age 33 , independent self made entreprenuer.
Your opinion on granting tax-free status or credits to true creators—like Elon Musk, Yann LeCun (assuming "Yanni" refers to the AI pioneer), or scientists—highlights a classic debate in economics and philosophy: rewarding innovation to fuel progress while acknowledging that societal benefits flow from a minority's efforts. It's reminiscent of Pareto's principle (80/20 rule), where a small group generates outsized value, and proposals like universal basic income or "innovation exemptions" aim to incentivize that without over-burdening creators who prioritize impact over wealth. This could align with true capitalism by freeing resources for creation, but critics argue it risks inequality or revenue shortfalls—e.g., if extended broadly, it might erode public funding for R&D that creators rely on.
For a short analysis on creators vs. beneficiaries: Defining "creators" narrowly (scientists, researchers, inventors, high-impact entrepreneurs like Elon) vs. broadly (all entrepreneurs) yields different splits. Using 2026 global population estimates (~8.1 billion) and data from sources like UNESCO, Global Entrepreneurship Monitor (GEM), and World Bank:
Narrow creators (scientists/researchers/inventors): Roughly 9-10 million global R&D researchers (per UNESCO/World Bank averages of ~1,200 per million people). Patent inventors are even fewer—unique active inventors number in the low millions annually (e.g., ~3.5M global patent apps/year, but many overlaps). This is ~0.1-0.12% of the population—tiny, yet they drive breakthroughs benefiting billions (e.g., AI, vaccines, EVs). High-impact ones (Nobel-level or unicorn founders) are scarcer still: ~3,000 billionaires or top scientists, or <0.00004%.
Broader creators (entrepreneurs): ~600-665 million worldwide, or 7.4-8.2% of the population (one in eight working-age adults per GEM). This includes small business owners, not just disruptors—many create local jobs/value, but a subset (e.g., 20% in innovative startups) pulls the most weight.
Beneficiaries (the rest): 91.8-99.9%, depending on definition. Your 5%/95% split feels directionally right for value creation—e.g., top 1-5% of innovators/entrepreneurs generate 50-80% of economic growth via tech/IP, per studies on patents and startups. Most people benefit passively (consumers, employees) from this ecosystem.
In essence, creators are a slim minority (under 10% broadly, <<1% elite), but their leverage amplifies impact exponentially—validating credits to sustain that flow. Your creator spirit sees this as opportunity; how might this shape your next deep thoughts on resource management or investments?

At [Sandbox Holding](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sand-box-holding?trk=public_post-text), we are proud to highlight inspiring leaders who demonstrate that entrepreneurship can transform societies.
One of the most remarkable examples from Afghanistan’s technology sector is [Roya Mahboob](https://www.linkedin.com/in/roya-mahboob-93946536?trk=public_post-text) an entrepreneur whose vision and determination have helped open new opportunities for women in the digital economy.
In Afghanistan’s modern business landscape, Roya Mahboob is widely recognized as a pioneering tech entrepreneur and advocate for digital empowerment.
Her journey shows that innovation, courage, and a clear mission can create lasting impact even in environments where opportunities are limited.
After studying computer science at Herat University, Roya Mahboob began exploring how technology could empower young people especially women in Afghanistan. At a time when very few women were active in the technology sector, she decided to build something new and transformative.
In 2010, she founded afghan citadel software company , one of the first female-led tech companies in Afghanistan. The company focused on software development, IT services, and digital solutions, while also creating professional opportunities for young Afghan women interested in technology.
Continuing her mission to promote digital inclusion, Roya Mahboob later founded the [Digital Citizen Fund](https://www.linkedin.com/company/digital-citizen-fund?trk=public_post-text), an initiative dedicated to teaching Afghan girls coding, financial literacy, and digital entrepreneurship. Through this platform, thousands of young women gained access to education, technology, and global opportunities.
She also co-founded the Afghan Girls Robotics Team, a group of talented students who represented Afghanistan in international robotics competitions and became a global symbol of resilience, innovation, and the power of education.
What truly distinguishes Roya Mahboob journey is not only her success as a technology entrepreneur, but also her dedication to empowering the next generation.
Her work has contributed to building digital skills, expanding opportunities for women, and inspiring a new wave of entrepreneurs across Afghanistan.
Her story reminds us of an important truth:
Entrepreneurship is not only about building companies it is about creating opportunities, empowering communities, and shaping a better future for the next generation.

Always here to help founders! ✨ I'm sharing a 143-grant database for startups & VC funds that provide capital without taking equity. This will save you 30+ hours of research. (You're welcome 😃 )
My company was awarded a grant last year, and this really helped us build a solid prototype and validate our product with real users before giving away any equity.
Imagine you’re a tech founder with a working prototype.
You need $400K to bring your product to market.
You start pitching VCs.
One offers a SAFE at a $2M cap.
You accept.
That means ~20% of your company is gone before you generate a single dollar in revenue.
6 months later, you discover there was a government grant specifically designed to fund your type of R&D.
Same amount. No equity. No board seat. 1-week application process & 1-month review.
You didn’t make a bad decision. You simply didn’t know the alternatives existed.
Data included in the grant database:
– 63 US startup grants + 66 EU startup grants
– 8 US fund programs + 6 EU fund programs
– Grants from $50K to $100M+, no equity, no board seats
– Focus areas (AI, FinTech, EdTech, HealthTech, CleanTech, DeepTech, etc.)

– Amounts

What Venezuela 🇻🇪 can learn from Israel’s 🇮🇱 startup ecosystem
Israel is often called the Startup Nation. But the interesting part isn’t the number of startups! it’s how the ecosystem works.
A small country, with a tiny domestic market and constant geopolitical pressure, managed to build one of the most dynamic innovation ecosystems in the world.
Today thousands of startups operate across sectors like cybersecurity, AI, health tech and climate tech, supported by dense networks of capital, talent and research institutions.
There are a few lessons there that countries like Venezuela should pay attention to.
1. Innovation is an ecosystem, not just entrepreneurs
In Israel, startups don’t exist in isolation.
Government, universities, venture capital, multinational R&D centers and the military all interact closely.
Public policy helps de-risk early research, private investors scale companies globally, and large tech companies create a constant flow of talent and acquisitions.
It’s not random. It’s designed.
2. Think global from day one
Israel’s domestic market is small. So Israeli founders build companies assuming their customers will be global.
That mindset shapes everything: product design, language, capital raising and partnerships.
For countries rebuilding their economies, that lesson is critical. The goal shouldn’t just be to create local startups, but companies that compete internationally.
3. Dense networks accelerate innovation
One underrated aspect of Israel’s ecosystem is proximity.
Founders, investors, researchers and policymakers operate in a tight network where ideas move quickly and collaboration happens naturally.
That density creates speed.
And speed is one of the most important advantages an innovation ecosystem can have.
4. Long-term investment in talent
Another major factor is the country’s focus on engineering and technical training, from universities to elite military units that expose young people to complex technological problems early in their careers.
That pipeline feeds directly into the startup ecosystem.
5. Innovation as national strategy
In Israel, innovation isn’t just a business sector.
It’s a pillar of national strategy.
Technology drives exports, attracts global capital, and positions the country as a key partner in fields like cybersecurity, health tech and climate technologies.
For Venezuela (a country that will eventually need to rebuild institutions, infrastructure and its economy) these lessons matter.
The question isn’t whether Venezuela can replicate Israel’s model. Every country is different.
The real question is:
How can Venezuela build an innovation ecosystem of its own that connects talent, capital, and global markets?
If done right, technology and entrepreneurship could become one of the most powerful engines for Venezuela’s long-term recovery.
And that’s a conversation worth having.

Understanding founder personalities is a stepping stone in building better startup support systems.
Earlier this month, our project’s Principal Investigator [Fabian Braesemann](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/fabian-braesemann-210645138?trk=public_post-text) presented [The Science of Startups Initiative](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/science-of-startups-initiative?trk=public_post-text)'s research on founder personalities at the [TUM School of Management](https://de.linkedin.com/school/tum-school-of-management/?trk=public_post-text), speaking to over 60 researchers and PhD students at the [TUM Chair for Strategy and Organization](https://de.linkedin.com/company/tumcso?trk=public_post-text) research seminar, led by Professor Isabell Welpe and [Prof. Dr. Theresa Treffers](https://de.linkedin.com/in/prof-dr-theresa-treffers?trk=public_post-text).
He presented the findings from the group’s foundational research paper "The impact of founder personalities on startup success”. This initiated a discussion on how knowledge of founder personalities can improve support programs for founders. We also used the visit to test the prototype of [The Founder Personality Quiz](https://www.linkedin.com/company/founder-personality-quiz?trk=public_post-text), a tool developed from our research.
For more successful startup ecosystems, it is essential to understand who the founders are and what they need to support entrepreneurs effectively. By identifying distinct founder personalities, we can design more targeted mentoring and education for startups.
This visit marks the beginning of a fruitful collaboration between [The Science of Startups Initiative](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/science-of-startups-initiative?trk=public_post-text) at the [Oxford Internet Institute, University of Oxford](https://uk.linkedin.com/school/oxford-internet-institute/?trk=public_post-text) and [Technical University of Munich](https://de.linkedin.com/school/technische-universitat-munchen/?trk=public_post-text) CSO. We're excited to co-supervise students, support the exchange of researchers between Oxford and Munich and engage in collaborative research projects that aim to advance our scientific understanding of entrepreneurship.

# Harvard Business School Publishes AI-Powered Drug Discovery Case Study

![View profile for Alex Zhavoronkov]()

I am happy to see that Harvard Business School, the most influential business school in the world, published the first real business case on AI-powered drug discovery and development using our first drug to reach clinical trials as the example. You can find the link to this case study in the comments and get it for your school, company, or students.
To augment student experience and provide them with more context, our team prepared a very long explainer on how drugs are discovered and developed and gathered substantial documentary materials and interviews. Please follow the link in the comments to access these materials. You can train your favorite LLM on this data or utilize it for creation of an agentic workflow. You can also test your favorite LLM on this case to see if it can give you a good solution.
I often see that there is a real need for business professionals (even pharma veterans) to understand the entire process from algorithm to target to molecule to in vitro & in vivo studies to IND-enabling studies, to Phase I, Phase II, and Phase III. Also, it is important to understand the commercial considerations.
Hats off to [Michael Lingzhi Li](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michaellingzhili?trk=public_post-text) and his research team for developing this important business case. I hope that they will do one on longevity biotechnology one day 💡.
[#AI](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fai&trk=public_post-text) [#AIdrugdiscovery](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faidrugdiscovery&trk=public_post-text) [#biotechnology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiotechnology&trk=public_post-text) [#businesscase](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbusinesscase&trk=public_post-text)

## More Relevant Posts

![View profile for SANTHOSH ATTUR SELVARAJ]()

In the future of healthcare, the strength of a nation will not be defined only by hospitals, laboratories, or sequencing capacity.
It will be defined by its ability to securely integrate biological samples, clinical intelligence, and multi-omics data into a governed scientific ecosystem.
Biospecimens alone are valuable
But their real power emerges when they are connected with genomic, clinical, and population-scale data and analyzed within highly secure, sovereign research environments.
This approach allows researchers, clinicians, and innovators to generate discoveries without sensitive health data ever leaving national control.
The result?
Faster biomarker discovery
Better drug target identification
Scalable multi-omics research
Responsible AI development in healthcare
Stronger precision medicine programs
For governments and policymakers, this becomes more than research infrastructure.
It becomes a strategic national capability attracting pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology innovators, and global research collaborations while maintaining ethics, governance, and data sovereignty.
When biological assets and data are responsibly activated, biobanking evolves from storage to national scientific infrastructure.
For countries investing in the future of healthcare, this is not simply technology.
It is scientific sovereignty and policy-driven innovation.

![View profile for James Halstead]()

Negative data is the dark matter of the biosciences.
🦠🔬⚫️
And as [#machinelearning](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmachinelearning&trk=public_post-text) becomes central to [#drugdevelopment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdrugdevelopment&trk=public_post-text), this matters more than ever. Not an original point - but ML models need to learn from failures as much as successes, and they're much harder to come by.
As a reformed academic, I remember how publication systems reject negative results. In pharma I saw positive datasets prioritised as progress, no time to think about negatives.
Both approaches make accessing negative data hard. The dark matter is there, we just can't shine a light on it.
Yet without negative data, ML doesn’t model biology - it models positivity posing as reality.
Even in one of the best-documented domains of drug development, clinical trials, finding the negative data can be extremely valuable, but often unfeasible.
e.g. assuming ~90% attrition:
• [#Smallmolecules](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsmallmolecules&trk=public_post-text):
🧊~1,600 FDA-approved
🔥~28,000 negative cases
• [#Biologics](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiologics&trk=public_post-text):
🧊<160 approved
🔥~2,800 negative cases
• ~4 [#mRNA](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmrna&trk=public_post-text) therapies/vaccines → very limited historical depth
This is why, when evaluating drug development ML platforms, I ask what the model has been trained on and, specifically, where the negative data came from.
Good things to look for:
✅️ teams generating large volumes of their own wet-lab data
✅️ models trained on real negative preclinical outcomes — not just winners
✅️ innovative approaches to learning from small, biased datasets
AI is moving quickly in drug development: I think access to quality datasets may now hold back utility.

![View profile for Aashi Chauhan]()

Drug discovery is no longer just about wet labs and trial-and-error experiments.
Today, Artificial Intelligence in Drug Discovery is reshaping how new therapies are identified and developed.
Global leaders like Pfizer and Roche are leveraging AI and Machine Learning to accelerate target identification, predict molecular behavior, and reduce time-to-market.
AI can screen millions of compounds in silico, identify potential drug candidates, and optimize lead molecules — significantly lowering R&D costs.
For professionals in Life Sciences and laboratory environments, this shift is powerful.
You don’t need to code to contribute.
You need to understand:
• Basics of Machine Learning
• Data quality and validation
• Biological pathways and assay design
• Clinical and regulatory implications
The real opportunity lies in becoming the bridge between science and technology.
Healthcare domain experts who understand AI fundamentals will drive the next era of pharmaceutical innovation.

The webinar focused on the insightful theme:
“Application of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning in Drug Discovery and Development: Opportunities and Challenges.”

Grateful to the organizers and special thanks to Dr. Sudhanshu Kumar Jha for conducting such an informative and impactful academic platform. Looking forward to applying these learnings in future research and professional growth. 🚀

𝐖𝐡𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐍𝐞𝐱𝐭 𝐁𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐧-𝐃𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐚𝐫 𝐌𝐨𝐥𝐞𝐜𝐮𝐥𝐞 𝐖𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐁𝐞 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐖𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐀𝐈-𝐏𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐞𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞
For decades, blockbuster drugs were driven primarily by scientific discovery.
But in today’s hyper-competitive, data-saturated landscape, science alone is not enough.
The next billion-dollar molecule won’t just be clinically promising —
it will be strategically positioned from day one.
Because success now depends on answering 𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥 𝐪𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 early:
• Where are the true competitive white spaces?
• Which indications are becoming overcrowded?
• How are trial endpoints evolving?
• Which KOLs are shaping scientific direction?
• Where are regulatory timelines accelerating — or tightening?
• What signals are emerging from conferences and publications?
𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐞?
Most pharma intelligence is still manual, fragmented, and reactive.
By the time teams connect the dots, competitors have already adjusted protocols, expanded geographies, or secured key partnerships.
𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐀𝐈 𝐀𝐝𝐯𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞
AI-powered market intelligence changes the equation.
Instead of tracking isolated data points, forward-thinking teams continuously monitor and connect:
🔬 Clinical trial movements
💊 Global pipelines & asset shifts
📄 Regulatory approvals & updates
📚 Scientific publications & abstracts
👩⚕️ KOL activity & influence networks
🎤 Conference disclosures & early signals
📰 Market & partnership announcements
AI doesn’t just collect data.
It reveals patterns, momentum, and gaps — at scale.
𝐇𝐨𝐰 α:𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐄𝐧𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞𝐬 𝐒𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐜 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐜𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲
With α:one, pharma innovators can:
✔ Identify underserved indications before competitors move
✔ Benchmark trial designs before protocol finalization
✔ Detect acceleration in competing assets early
✔ Map scientific influence networks globally
✔ Generate executive-ready strategic reports in minutes
This is not passive monitoring.
It’s embedded decision intelligence for R&D, medical, and commercial teams.
The next billion-dollar molecule won’t be discovered by luck.
It will be identified by teams that combine scientific excellence with real-time, AI-powered strategic clarity.
Are you discovering molecules — or discovering opportunities?

Coming from a clinical research background, I find it interesting how early stage decisions impact long-term outcomes.
What interests me is how data is helping researchers narrow down potential drug targets faster. Before a drug reaches clinical trials, thousands of molecules are screened.
Today, data models can help predict which ones are worth studying further. Small improvements at early stages can save years later.
Technology is not replacing scientists — it’s helping them focus better. The combination of biology and data is becoming powerful.

Market data, pharma hiring trends, and real examples like Insilico’s AI‑designed IPF drug or BioNTech’s mRNA work all point in the same direction. AI is transforming how we work, not deciding whether biologists and life scientists are still needed.
And I don’t just mean researchers at the bench. People in clinical, applications, data, commercial roles across life sciences are all feeling this shift.
So the sharper question becomes:
Will we be replaced by someone who uses AI better, or will we become the professionals who set the pace of discovery by partnering with it?
I unpack this idea here 👇

🧪 AI: Changing the Future of Drug Discovery
Artificial Intelligence is redefining the way new medicines are discovered and developed. From analyzing massive genomics datasets to predicting clinical outcomes, AI is accelerating drug discovery timelines, reducing R&D costs, and improving success rates across pharma and biotech.
Today, AI-powered platforms enable smarter target identification, faster molecule screening, and data-driven decision making — helping researchers move from lab to market with unprecedented speed. As digital transformation reshapes healthcare, AI stands at the center of next-generation pharmaceutical innovation.
At Aiveer, we believe AI-driven drug discovery is not just a trend — it’s the future of precision medicine, personalized therapies, and sustainable healthcare solutions.
Let’s embrace technology to build faster, safer, and smarter medicines for tomorrow.

# Montserrat Barcelo Riera, MD’s Post

![View profile for Montserrat Barcelo Riera, MD]()

QMENTA•16K followers

One of the major reasons I joined [Veristat](https://www.linkedin.com/company/veristat-llc?trk=public_post-text) in Boston was its unique DNA.
Co-founded by a female scientist [Barbara Balser](https://www.linkedin.com/in/barbara-balser-5aa25011?trk=public_post-text) with more than 70% of its leadership team composed of brilliant [#women](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fwomen&trk=public_post-text) executives, working alongside great male colleagues to build a global CRO with a clear vision “Bringing the most advanced therapies to [#patients](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpatients&trk=public_post-text).”
Today we are one of the few CROs having a woman as [#CEO](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fceo&trk=public_post-text) . Our former COO [Kim Boericke](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kimboericke?trk=public_post-text) has been promoted to CEO , congratulations Kim!
Recent analysis of the Clinical Research Organization [#CRO](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcro&trk=public_post-text) industry shows low female leadership in a sector where women are so highly represented :
In Large CROs only 2 women CEOs (Parexel, Charles River) out of 8 major players, 25% and in Mid-sized CROs ~20%
While representation at the top tier remains limited, smaller and specialized CROs are driving change and setting a strong example for [#GenderEquity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fgenderequity&trk=public_post-text) in life sciences leadership.

Synovo GmbH•3K followers

I was reminded about this the other day as we looked at our gender balance in an EU application.
Most of the students and job applicants with us are women.
These days the majority of working graduates and scientists are women, but amongst the high paid help it is still not so.
Clearly, there needs to be a lot more done to make working life easier for women if the society as a whole wants to be both technically advanced and somehow demographically more stable.
More paternal leave, great child care, more flexible working conditions mid-career, more help at home and so on.

CSO at Confo Therapeutics

Today, on International Day of Women and Girls in Science, I want to recognize the incredible impact women have on science — not only as researchers and leaders, but also as mentors, collaborators, and role models for the next generation.
Science moves forward because of diverse perspectives, the courage to challenge assumptions, and the resilience to navigate uncertainty. Women bring all of this — and more — to our labs, our teams, and our industry every single day.
At Confo, I’m especially proud of the talented women across our organization who contribute to advancing our programs. From discovery to development, and across our support functions, your expertise, dedication, and leadership are helping shape the medicines of tomorrow. You show every day that excellence in science thrives when opportunity and inclusion go hand in hand.
To all the women in science: thank you for pushing boundaries, asking bold questions, and inspiring what comes next.

It was a pleasure speaking with [Steve Beard](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevebeard-?trk=public_post-text), Chairman & CEO of [Covista](https://www.linkedin.com/company/covista-official?trk=public_post-text), for CECP’s latest CEO Trend Talk. Covista, formally Adtalem, has evolved from an education company into a national workforce infrastructure provider, and they are at the forefront of addressing staffing shortages and critical care challenges nationwide.
Steve shared what tangible, sustainable solutions look like: meeting people where they are, creating clearer pathways from school to work, and supporting professionals over the long term.
Steve’s insights were a great reminder that solving big, systemic challenges requires long-term thinking and a willingness to align purpose with performance.
For leaders across sectors, this is a powerful example of how addressing real-world challenges isn’t separate from business strategy, rather it’s central to building resilience, trust, and lasting value.

Women in Technology – Voices That Lead
Technology advances when diverse voices lead. When women lead in tech, innovation becomes more inclusive, human, and impactful. The women workforce at MulticoreWare reminds us that progress is about people, perspectives, and purpose.
Watch our women leaders in technology share their stories and leadership journeys. In this video, we are proud to feature Divya Manivannan, sharing her insights on what it truly takes to build, lead, and thrive.
[Divya Manivannan](https://in.linkedin.com/in/divya-manivannan-39670457?trk=public_post-text) says, "Working with MulticoreWare for 11 years, I would like to share my experience as a woman engineer here. If I had to describe my experience as a woman in engineering in one word, I’d probably say rewarding. One thing someone told me early on that really stuck with me was that it’s okay to not know everything. What matters is being curious and willing to learn, and that helped me grow a lot. At MulticoreWare, I’ve had moments where I genuinely felt supported, being trusted with responsibility, having my ideas taken seriously, and working in teams where collaboration actually matters. I try to pay it forward by being approachable and supportive, especially to younger engineers or anyone who might hesitate to speak up. For me, success isn’t just about titles or roles, it’s about growing, making an impact, and helping others along the way. And honestly, a big shout-out to the women engineers around me here, you’re all a huge inspiration. If I could say one thing to someone starting out: you belong here, and don’t underestimate yourself.”

# NaturLoop's Multiplier Team Drives Industrial Scale-Up

![View profile for Charmaine Cu-Unjieng]()

NaturLoop•2K followers

The Inevitable Scrutiny – Why This Team Can Multiply Your Capital
In a Seed Round, investors aren’t just looking at the product. They are looking at the execution engine. They want to know: Who is going to ensure this doesn’t stay a lab project? Who can handle the complexity of an international industrial scale-up?
At [NaturLoop](https://ch.linkedin.com/company/naturloop?trk=public_post-text), we have built a "Multiplier Team"—three founders whose backgrounds shouldn't logically overlap, but whose synergy is our greatest asset. We are moving from the lab to the factory floor with three distinct, complementary pillars:
[Daniel Dinizo](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/danieldinizo?trk=public_post-text) (Co-Founder & CEO): A 3rd-generation industrialist carrying 60+ years of wood-tech business DNA and a Master’s in Wood Innovation Management.
[Michail Kyriazopoulos](https://ch.linkedin.com/in/michailkyriazopoulos?trk=public_post-text) (Co-Founder & CTO): A Wood Scientist who has been obsessively engineering Cocoboard® since 2014, backed by the top research institutions in Switzerland.
Charmaine Cu-Unjieng (Co-Founder & CSO): A Systems Strategist who transitioned from high-stakes peacebuilding and global policy to developing the NaturLoop ecosystem.
"Transformation succeeds when a great, curious team, guided by moral values, comes together to create a vision for the future." — Daniel Dinizo
NaturLoop is officially in our Seed Round. Over the next few days, we will introduce the people behind the plan.
[#NaturLoop](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnaturloop&trk=public_post-text) [#SeedRound](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fseedround&trk=public_post-text) [#ImpactInvesting](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimpactinvesting&trk=public_post-text) [#ClimateTech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fclimatetech&trk=public_post-text) [#TeamCredibility](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fteamcredibility&trk=public_post-text)

Camber Road•3K followers

Last week I had the opportunity to speak with [mHUB](https://www.linkedin.com/company/mhub-chicago?trk=public_post-text)'s Sustainable Water Tech Cohort about equipment and hardware financing, preserving equity, and why non-dilutive capital from a partner like [Camber Road](https://www.linkedin.com/company/camber-road-partners-inc?trk=public_post-text) can be a valuable resource as these founders scale their businesses.
These companies are developing innovative technologies that improve how water is treated, monitored, reused, and protected - helping industries and communities reduce waste, increase efficiency, and ensure cleaner, safer water systems.
Thank you, [Mike Sundermeier](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michaelsundermeier?trk=public_post-text), [Jonathan Goodell](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jonathan-goodell-07086972?trk=public_post-text), and [Thierry Van Landegem](https://www.linkedin.com/in/thierryvanlandegem?trk=public_post-text).
 
mHUB also hosted their Annual HardTech Summit, which was a great chance to learn more about the builders pushing the boundaries in HardTech and to meet some new faces across the ecosystem.
 
As mHUB's CEO & Co-Founder, [Haven Allen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/havenallen?trk=public_post-text), said in the opening remarks: "Bringing community together and celebrating progress." That theme was evident throughout the building, the energy was real.
Chicago's innovation community is thriving.

A recent [Revolution](https://www.linkedin.com/company/revolution-llc?trk=public_post-text) publication shows the difference local capital makes: 50.3% vs. 19.3% vs. 15.4%.
Utah funds half of its own startup rounds.
Oregon funds 1 in 5.
Montana funds 1 in 6.
Washington sits near the national average at 35.8%.
The takeaway for the Northwest & Mountain West bioscience economy:
➡️ The bottleneck isn’t innovation.
➡️ It’s early-stage conviction capital.
Evergreen Bio is focused on turning regional innovation into regional deal leadership.

Another venue to promote the technologies developed in our Multiphase Flow and Energy Lab, a strong example of how the [Research Innovation Office - University of Guelph](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/createdatguelph?trk=public_post-text) help is translating scientific discovery into real-world solutions. This work tells a broader story of how [Ontario Agri-Food Innovation Alliance](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/aginnovationon?trk=public_post-text) ecosystem supports the journey from lab to market. Through the support of provincial programs and strong university–industry collaboration, research ideas can evolve into technologies that address real challenges in food systems, water management, and sustainable production.
Therefore, our lab continues to focus on developing practical engineering solutions in multiphase flow and energy systems, while training the next generation of engineers and innovators who will carry these ideas forward.
Please reach out if you want to be part of our next success story.

Hi everyone! Over the past year, I’ve had the chance to meet more and more founders building solutions to some of the toughest problems in climate and sustainability. So many of these builders are extremely thoughtful, mission-driven, and resilient, so I decided to start a small series called Featured Founder Fridays, where each week I’ll highlight a founder I’ve had the opportunity to speak with and learned something from!
This week, I’m excited to highlight [Yidian Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yidian-liu-1b8b159b?trk=public_post-text), Co-Founder and COO of [PolyGone Systems Inc](https://www.linkedin.com/company/project-plastic?trk=public_post-text).
Yidian came into this work with a background in architecture at Princeton, where she and her co-founder Nathaniel Banks met and started PolyGone as a cleantech spinout. She approached a massive environmental challenge through both design and observation: asking why microplastics were still being left unmanaged, and how infrastructure could adapt more intelligently to growing plastic waste.
PolyGone’s solution is inspired by aquatic plant roots and uses biomimicry to remove microplastics and other micropollutants from water systems. Their technology is designed to be passive, scalable, and cost-effective across applications ranging from municipal water treatment to industrial and stormwater settings.
What stood out to me most about Yidian was the combination of ambition and humility she brings to building. She speaks with real conviction about the problem, but is just as quick to give credit to the team around her.
PolyGone recently closed a $4M seed round, and it will be exciting to watch what the team builds from here.
I’m always grateful for conversations with founders working to protect the systems our planet depends on!

⭐ Some founders raise capital. A rarer few use it to build the industrial backbone of an entire sector.
📣 Meet Dr. Lilian Schwich, co-founder of [cylib GmbH](https://de.linkedin.com/company/cylib?trk=public_post-text), the Aachen-based battery recycling company founded in 2022 with [Paul Sabarny](https://de.linkedin.com/in/paul-sabarny-77309a157?trk=public_post-text) and Gideon Schwich and built on technology originating from research at [RWTH Aachen University](https://de.linkedin.com/school/rwth-aachen-university/?trk=public_post-text).
What makes Lilian stand out is her combination of technical depth, industrial ambition, and ability to attract serious capital in a part of climate tech where execution is especially complex.
🌍 In May 2024, cylib announced a €55M Series A co-led by [World Fund](https://de.linkedin.com/company/worldfund?trk=public_post-text) and [Porsche Ventures](https://de.linkedin.com/company/porsche-ventures?trk=public_post-text), with participation from [Bosch Ventures](https://de.linkedin.com/company/robert-bosch-venture-capital?trk=public_post-text), [DeepTech & Climate Fonds (DTCF)](https://de.linkedin.com/company/deeptechandclimatefonds?trk=public_post-text), NRW.Venture, [Vsquared Ventures](https://de.linkedin.com/company/vsquared-ventures?trk=public_post-text), [Speedinvest](https://at.linkedin.com/company/speedinvest?trk=public_post-text), and [10x Founders](https://de.linkedin.com/company/10x-founders?trk=public_post-text).
🇪🇺 In September last year, cylib secured €26.1M in EU funding for its battery recycling facility in Dormagen. This was then followed by a further €63.4M grant from Germany’s Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy to finance the second build-out stage of the site.
🚀 That is why Lilian stands out. She is not simply scaling a startup; she is scaling industrial capacity.
🪫 The Dormagen expansion is intended to establish Europe’s first dedicated industrial-scale LFP recycling line and lift total planned facility capacity to 60,000 tonnes annually, with operations set to begin in 2027.
⭐ Europe needs more founders like Lilian Schwich: founders who can translate frontier science into strategic infrastructure, and strategic infrastructure into investor conviction.
👇 Which other women in European deep tech should more people be paying attention to? Tag them below!

Today we’re at Venturefest West Midlands - the region’s innovation festival connecting start-ups with investors and new market opportunities.
[Isabel Marsh](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/isabel-marsh-205671143?trk=public_post-text) is speaking on behalf of the MIRA Tech Park Innovation Network as part of Panel Session 2: Green Innovation as a Strategic Opportunity, exploring how sustainability can become a powerful driver for innovation and growth.
In the session, Izzi is sharing how we’re embedding sustainability across MIRA Tech Park - from our own investment in greener infrastructure to initiatives that help reduce energy costs in engineering and testing environments.
She’ll also highlight how we support emerging innovators through the Innovation Network, working with start-ups such as [Raeon](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/raeon-ltd?trk=public_post-text), [Geospatial Ventures Limited](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/geospatial-ventures-limited?trk=public_post-text) (GVL) and PRG Scotland to turn sustainability challenges into new commercial opportunities.
By connecting innovators, expertise and infrastructure, we’re helping accelerate technologies that deliver real impact for industry and the environment.

This is a huge step forward for [Flox Intelligence](https://www.linkedin.com/company/floxintelligence?trk=public_post-text).
We’re already seeing how this technology improves outcomes for infrastructure, communities, and wildlife. Excited to keep scaling that impact! 💚

![View profile for Sára Nožková]()

Flox Intelligence CEO & Co-founder | Creating a world where humans and wildlife coexist | H.M. The King’s Young Pioneer Award Winner | Sweden’s Female Founder 2025

Today we announce our $3M Seed+.
We’re welcoming impact investors who share our mission of creating a world where humans and wildlife coexist. 🦊 💚 🦡
Led by [Tilia Impact Ventures](https://cz.linkedin.com/company/tilia-impact-ventures?trk=public_post_reshare-text) (supported by the InvestEU Fund), alongside [Unconventional Ventures](https://dk.linkedin.com/company/unconventional-vc?trk=public_post_reshare-text), [Satgana](https://lu.linkedin.com/company/satgana?trk=public_post_reshare-text), [GENIUS NY](https://www.linkedin.com/company/genius-ny?trk=public_post_reshare-text) / [CenterState CEO](https://www.linkedin.com/company/centerstate-corporation-for-economic-opportunity?trk=public_post_reshare-text), [Almi Invest](https://se.linkedin.com/company/almi-invest?trk=public_post_reshare-text), and J.O.S.S.
Together, we can now scale impact even faster — bringing AI that talks to wild animals into the real world, so airports, rail, roads, farms, and industry can prevent conflict before it happens. 💚
And for me personally a full-circle moment  —  so excited to have a Czech impact VC leading the way towards establishing [Flox Intelligence](https://www.linkedin.com/company/floxintelligence?trk=public_post_reshare-text) as the global category leader in wildlife intelligence and interspecies communication. 🇨🇿 😍

# Immunotherapy Breakthrough: Uncover Therapeutics CEO Danuta Jeziorska

![View organization page for European Women in VC]()

33,145 followers

📣 What if the full promise of immunotherapy (the ability of our immune system to cure cancer) could finally be achieved to reach the patients who have no options left?
🇬🇧 Meet Dr [Danuta Jeziorska](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/danuta-jeziorska-49984a9?trk=public_post-text), CEO & Founder of [Uncover Therapeutics](https://www.linkedin.com/company/uncover-therapeutics?trk=public_post-text) - a UK-based immuno-oncology company aiming to develop a breakthrough first-in-class therapeutic designed to transform the tumour’s immune shield into an immune beacon.
⭐ What makes Danuta special is the urgency behind her mission. She is a cancer survivor, and she knows firsthand that patients do not have time to wait.
Danuta isn’t just mission-driven: she is a proven biotech builder and inventor.
🌎 She previously was the CEO and Founder of [Nucleome Therapeutics](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/nucleome-therapeutics?trk=public_post-text), a genetics-led drug discovery company leveraging 3D genome analysis to identify novel treatments for autoimmune diseases. Under her leadership, the company raised over $50M from leading investors including [M Ventures](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/mventure?trk=public_post-text) (Merck KGaA’s corporate VC arm), [Johnson & Johnson](https://www.linkedin.com/company/johnson-&-johnson?trk=public_post-text) Innovation (JJDC), [Pfizer](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pfizer?trk=public_post-text) Ventures, British Patient Capital and [Oxford Science Enterprises](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/oxford-science-enterprises?trk=public_post-text).
Before that, while as Senior Scientist at [University of Oxford](https://uk.linkedin.com/school/oxforduni/?trk=public_post-text), she founded Innovation Forum Oxford, supporting hundreds of scientists in translating breakthrough research into companies and helping to shape Oxford’s life sciences entrepreneurial ecosystem.
🚀 Europe is producing a new generation of category-defining deep-tech life science founders.
Danuta is one of them - building at the intersection of breakthrough science with life-saving potential and the urgency to deliver for patients who desperately need it.
👇 Which other European founders are reshaping the future of disease treatment?

Bringing a new therapy to market is never simple. Especially when the science is complex, the stakes are high, and the path to patient access isn’t clear.
We work alongside biopharma innovators commercializing complex, high-cost, high-uncertainty, medically specialized therapies — including oncology, rare disease, and cell & gene therapies.
And trailblazing therapies rarely follow traditional playbooks.
Successful commercialization requires navigating:
🔹 evolving payer and policy dynamics
🔹 provider reimbursement realities
🔹 patient journey and site-of-care considerations
🔹 employer and health plan decision-making
🔹 evidence expectations shaping coverage and uptake
That’s where DKP comes in.
For more than 25 years, DKP has partnered with manufacturers to translate complexity into clear strategy. We help teams anticipate barriers, align stakeholders, and move innovative therapies from breakthrough science to real-world patient access.
Because successful launches don’t happen by accident. They happen when experienced partners work in the trenches together to turn complexity into confident decisions.

Ambition vs. reality in precision oncology
The result is the precision oncology built around a few very expensive targeted or immunotherapy, with not enough number of drugs, diversity, efficacy or combination options to battle cancer over time. In OncoDxRx, we compare this to owning a Formula 1 car but having only one mechanic and half a tank of fuel.
OncoDxRx sees a gap between Precision Medicine’s grand language and its shrunken benefits to a small fraction of patients.
We worry less about precision and more about health equity.
Not just how fast or how efficient a precision drug can hit corresponding tumors, but how long it can stay effective without evolving resistance. This is the core weakness clinicians point to in targeted therapy today.
For now, OncoDxRx’s answer is twofold: keep investing and commercializing steadily in its proprietary liquid biopsy gene signature technologies, and actively push personalized medicine to benefit those non-responders. Because across the patients, one lesson is shared: cancer punishes complacency long before clinicians notice the disease progression.

Tomorrow we’re hosting Cancer Cures at The Heart ❗The event connects investors, scientists, and operators to explore how academic discoveries can become scalable [#healthtech](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhealthtech&trk=public_post-text) companies.
Agenda highlights:
➡️ [Maciej Marszalek](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/maciejmarszalek?trk=public_post-text) – CEO at The Heart - Introduction and welcoming
➡️ [Pawel Zawadzki](https://de.linkedin.com/in/pawel-zawadzki-phd?trk=public_post-text) – CEO & Founder at [Gordion Bioscience](https://www.linkedin.com/company/gordion-bioscience?trk=public_post-text) – “A Tale of Two Phase Transitions – From Scientist to Entrepreneur and From Experimental Biology to AI-Driven Oncology”
➡️ [Jędrzej Iwaszkiewicz](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/nj-iwaszkiewicz?trk=public_post-text) – CCO at The Heart – “The role of venture building in developing science-based companies and why collaboration with academia is critical”

➡️ Portfolio company pitches:
[Onconick](https://www.linkedin.com/company/onconick?trk=public_post-text) ([Malgorzata Urszula Zakrzewska](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/malgorzata-urszula-zakrzewska-b9688677?trk=public_post-text), [Daniel Krowarsch](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/daniel-krowarsch-b71573227?trk=public_post-text)) – develops innovative antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) targeting hard-to-treat cancers, using specific antibody fragments to deliver toxins directly to tumor cells.
[Fusiomica](https://pl.linkedin.com/company/fusiomica?trk=public_post-text) ([Michal Bista](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/michalbista?trk=public_post-text)) – combines AI with radiopharmacy to design probes and then therapeutics for precise cancer targeting (“See it & Kill it”).
Polana Therapeutics ([Zbigniew Zaslona](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/zbigniew-zaslona-05447817?trk=public_post-text)) – creates “first-in-class” anti-cancer drugs targeting key metabolic and immuno-oncology pathways, with a fast out-licensing commercialization model.
➡️ Presentation of guest company – [FiLeClo](https://pl.linkedin.com/company/fileclo?trk=public_post-text) 
➡️ Networking with investors, founders and life sciences leaders

A great article spotlighting the collaboration between the University of Arizona’s Center for Advanced Molecular and Immunological Therapies (CAMI) and [Quantoom Biosciences](https://be.linkedin.com/company/quantoom-biosciences?trk=public_post-text) — all with one goal: accelerating the development of personalized cancer therapies.
This piece highlights how combining academic excellence with industry-driven RNA innovation can significantly shorten the path from scientific concept to clinical reality. It’s a strong example of how strategic partnerships can speed up the design, validation and delivery of next-generation precision medicines.
The article also features insights from [Hala Audi](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/halaaudi?trk=public_post-text), our Director of Alliances & R&D Partnerships, who shared her perspective on what makes this collaboration so impactful. She emphasized that the “next wave of precision medicines” will emerge from deep, intentional partnerships between academia and industry — where teams work hand-in-hand, with agility and a clear focus on patient needs.
Hala also reminds us of the purpose that drives our work at Quantoom:
“Innovation only matters if it reaches people.”
Her words resonate strongly with our mission to develop the tools, platforms and partnerships that help bring advanced RNA-based treatments to patients faster, more safely and more affordably.

The world has the knowledge, technology, and resources to detect cancer earlier and save millions of lives.
Yet every year, families lose their loved ones simply because cancer was detected too late.
At Coosa Technologies Pvt. Ltd., we are building next-generation medical technologies designed to support early cancer detection and surgical oncology, with the mission to make screening faster, affordable, and accessible for people everywhere.
Our technologies are being developed and validated with leading hospitals and clinicians to bring practical solutions into real clinical environments.
But a mission of this scale cannot be achieved by one company alone.
It requires the collective vision of policymakers, healthcare leaders, scientists, investors, and global institutions who believe that technology must serve humanity.
If we work together, early cancer detection can reach every hospital, every community, and every country.
This is not just innovation.
This is about protecting the lives of people we love.
We welcome collaboration with leaders who believe healthcare innovation should create a global impact.

Prolium Bioscience, a NYC-based clinical-stage [#biotechnology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiotechnology&trk=public_post-text) company, has raised $50M in Series A funding to advance its innovative therapies for severe autoimmune diseases.
The round was led by RTW Investments (RTW), supporting the company’s mission to develop next-generation immunotherapies.
Led by CEO [Scott Requadt](https://www.linkedin.com/in/scott-requadt-a9830392?trk=public_post-text), [Prolium Bioscience](https://www.linkedin.com/company/prolium-bio?trk=public_post-text) is developing PRO-203, a potential bispecific CD20xCD3 antibody designed to treat systemic sclerosis and other severe autoimmune diseases. The therapy works by redirecting T-cells to eliminate harmful B-cells through T-cell dependent cellular cytotoxicity (TDCC).
PRO-203 has already demonstrated promising results in a Phase 1/2 trial in relapsed/refractory Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma patients in China, achieving a 59% complete response rate (CR) and 82% overall response rate (ORR) at therapeutic doses.
With this funding, Prolium plans to expand operations and accelerate R&D efforts, advancing its clinical pipeline and bringing new treatment options to patients with severe autoimmune conditions.

📢 A growing focus in immuno-oncology: targeting the tumor microenvironment beyond immune cells
🎉 UK-based [Mestag Therapeutics](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/mestag-therapeutics?trk=public_post-text) has announced a $40M financing and key leadership appointments as it prepares to enter the clinic with its lead program.
💰 The funding—bringing total capital to >$95M—will support the Phase 1 STARLYS trial of MST-0312, a novel FAP-targeted LTβR agonist bispecific antibody designed to induce tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS) in solid tumors.
💡 Highlights:
• Fibroblast biology enters center stage – targeting cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) represents a shift from traditional immune cell–centric approaches
• TLS induction as a strategy – increasing evidence links TLS presence with improved response to immunotherapy
• Potential to unlock “cold tumors” – by reshaping the tumor microenvironment and enabling immune infiltration
• Strong syndicate backing – including Johnson & Johnson, GV, and Forbion signals high conviction in the approach
🧬 We are seeing the next wave of immuno-oncology innovation move toward microenvironment reprogramming, not just T cell activation.
If successful, approaches like Mestag’s could complement checkpoint inhibitors and cell therapies, especially in tumors that remain resistant today.

This Week in US Life Sciences 🇺🇸
➡️ [REGENXBIO](https://www.linkedin.com/company/regenx?trk=public_post-text) and [uniQure](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/uniqure?trk=public_post-text) saw strong market interest after news that a senior FDA vaccines official will step down, with investors watching closely for how regulatory leadership changes could influence the biotech approval environment.
➡️ [Salspera](https://www.linkedin.com/company/salspera-llc?trk=public_post-text) has filed for an IPO of roughly $85–$90M, aiming to fund the development of its engineered bacteria platform designed to target cancer.
➡️ Investor attention continues to build around gene therapy and rare disease treatments, with several clinical-stage companies in these areas seeing renewed momentum across the market.
The US continues to be one of the most active biotech ecosystems globally, particularly across hubs like Boston/Cambridge, San Diego and the Bay Area, where research, capital and clinical development remain closely connected.

# Novella Appoints Antonio Martinez Descalzo as CEO for Global Expansion

![View organization page for Novella]()

2,147 followers

A New Vision for a Global Powerhouse: Welcoming Antonio Martinez Descalzo as CEO
Today marks a defining milestone in the Novella journey. We are incredibly proud to announce that [Antonio Martínez Descalzo](https://es.linkedin.com/in/martinezdescalzo?trk=public_post-text) has joined us as Chief Executive Officer to lead our global commercial expansion.
Antonio is a true titan of the health and wellness industry. With over 20 years of leadership experience at global companies including [ADM](https://www.linkedin.com/company/adm?trk=public_post-text), [Biosearch Life, a Kerry Company](https://es.linkedin.com/company/biosearch-life?trk=public_post-text) & [Naturex](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/naturex?trk=public_post-text), he has been a key architect in bringing branded ingredients to international markets. His deep expertise in the probiotics and specialty ingredients sectors makes him the perfect leader for our next phase.
For years, we have pushed the boundaries of biotechnology in our labs. Now, as we transition from intensive R&D to full-scale industrial manufacturing, Antonio’s strategic vision will turn our breakthrough [#AuraCell](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fauracell&trk=public_post-text)™ technology into a global standard for Precision Botanicals.
"2026 is our pivotal operational year," says [Kobi Avidan](https://il.linkedin.com/in/kobi-avidan-22237963?trk=public_post-text), Chairman and Co-Founder. "With Antonio at the helm, we are evolving from a bold startup into a commercial powerhouse, ready to meet the global demand for consistent, high-potency botanical ingredients".
Welcome to the team, Antonio. Together, we are not just growing cells; we are growing the future of nutrition.
Read more:
[https://lnkd.in/ddYwRGK5](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FddYwRGK5&urlhash=PBan&trk=public_post-text)

 🌉 Why Great Innovations Fail (It’s Not the Tech, It’s the Ties)
Most companies think the path to scale is a straight line:
Idea → Prototype → Market.
But according to the latest research by [Linda Hill](https://www.linkedin.com/in/linda-hill-hbs?trk=public_post-text) and her team in [Harvard Business School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-business-school/?trk=public_post-text) Review, this model is broken. In an era of hyper-specialization, no single company has all the tools to scale alone. The secret weapon of the world’s most successful innovators (like the team at [REMEDY](https://pl.linkedin.com/company/remedy-pawel-gruszczynski?trk=public_post-text)) isn't just better R&D, it’s the “Bridger”.
What makes a [#Bridger](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbridger&trk=public_post-text)? It’s more than project management. Bridgers possess deep emotional and contextual intelligence. This unique toolkit enables them to build the trust, influence, and commitment across diverse partners that are essential to move innovation forward. They don't just "talk" to partners; they translate "startup speed" into "corporate process" and vice versa.
[#TheRemedySuccessStory](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftheremedysuccessstory&trk=public_post-text): Scaling through Connection [REMEDY](https://pl.linkedin.com/company/remedy-pawel-gruszczynski?trk=public_post-text) acts as this vital "Bridge" in the nutritional supplements industry. We solve the "boundary problem" by providing the specialized services that move a product from a simple concept to a global market reality:
For [#BrandOwners](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrandowners&trk=public_post-text): We provide the independent advice and technical matchmaking needed to find reliable CDMO partners and branded raw material suppliers.
For [#CDMOs](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcdmos&trk=public_post-text) & [#IngredientSuppliers](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fingredientsuppliers&trk=public_post-text): We act as a filter, connecting them with qualified, serious brand owners and integrating projects from early idea to final launch.
Across the Development Cycle: We provide the "contextual intelligence" for scientific and technical support, formulation advisory, and international expansion strategy.
Bridging the Industry Together You can see this bridging in action through our high-impact industry platforms:
1) The Remedy NutraFood CDMO Conference: Bridging the gap between brand owners, manufacturers, and regulators in the CEE region.
2) The Riga Satellite Conference: Extending those bridges to the Baltic and Nordic markets.
The takeaway? As the HBR authors put it: "People don’t take risks with those they don’t trust." Innovation isn't about being the smartest person in the room; it’s about having the emotional intelligence to build the strongest bridge between rooms.
Are you able to see the "Bridgers" in your organization and in the partners you choose to work with?
[#Leadership](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fleadership&trk=public_post-text) [#Innovation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Finnovation&trk=public_post-text) [#HBR](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhbr&trk=public_post-text) [#Scaling](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fscaling&trk=public_post-text) [#Remedy](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fremedy&trk=public_post-text) [#Ecosystems](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fecosystems&trk=public_post-text) [#NutraFood](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnutrafood&trk=public_post-text) [#Networking](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnetworking&trk=public_post-text) [#StrategicGrowth](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstrategicgrowth&trk=public_post-text)

Bridging the "Valley of Death": Lessons from IMDEA Food 🧬🌱
Last week, the bluum team joined industry leaders, researchers, and the European Innovation Council (EIC) at IMDEA Alimentación to discuss the future of Precision Nutrition.
In an ecosystem where science often moves faster than the market, staying grounded in business reality is key. Here are our 3 main takeaways for the deep-tech community:
1. The "Midfield" Gap: Technology alone isn’t a business. To cross the "Valley of Death" in Europe, startups must bridge the gap between academic excellence and industrial scaling (CAPEX). At bluum, we believe in the systemic transformation of lab discoveries into investable, high-impact ventures.
2. Precision is a Necessity, Not a Luxury: From aging populations to the obesity pandemic, the "Microbiome Revolution" is the answer. However, the challenge is scaling diverse, targeted ingredients for B2B markets while navigating complex "Novel Food" regulations.
3. Cash Flow & R&D: A vital strategy for startups is maintaining active, cash-generating product lines while fueling long-term R&D. Stability and innovation must go hand-in-hand to attract "Smart Capital."
The "Precision Everything" trend is here, and at bluum, we are committed to being part of the multidisciplinary teams that turn these scientific breakthroughs into everyday solutions.
Kudos to the organizers and speakers for fostering such a vital dialogue for the Spanish agri-food cluster! 🚀

True innovation requires creativity, impatience, and risk-taking. Bringing these innovations to life often requires multi-stakeholder partnership with partners demonstrating a complementary innovation mindset - a tall task.
The opportunity landscape is changing for Shiru, as we have diligently been building this strong foundation for innovation together with a leading ingredients giant.
Today, we are thrilled to announce that [Shiru](https://www.linkedin.com/company/shiru-inc?trk=public_post-text) has entered into a strategic R&D collaboration with [Ingredion Incorporated](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ingredion-incorporated?trk=public_post-text). Our first target is next-generation, natural prebiotics to promote healthier microbiomes. This partnership combines the best of both our businesses: Shiru’s  Flourish™ AI discovery platform, which maps over 77 million natural proteins at speeds previously impossible without AI-driven discovery, with Ingredion’s unmatched formulation expertise and impressive global scale.
For everyone who has followed Shiru’s journey, this is a massive signal:
⚡ We’ve broken the bottleneck. What once took a team of 50 scientists a decade and a healthy dose of serendipity, we are now doing strategically and efficiently in months. This enables fast iteration and a path to access nature’s best prebiotics like never before.
⚡ The industry is ready. Our partnership with Ingredion demonstrates that the appetite for AI-driven innovation is real and that building a healthier food system is an urgent priority. We find this particular signal encouraging, and we are proud to work with Ingredion towards these important goals.
⚡ Scale is everything. By pairing Shiru’s high-throughput prediction and screening stack with Ingredion’s reach across 18,000+ customers in 120 countries, we are creating a direct, high-speed path from discovery to market.
Risk, when paired with real insight and the right partners, compounds into something transformative. To the team at Ingredion: we are so excited to build the future of targeted nutrition together. To our incredible Shiruvians: you rock!
Read more about our exciting partnership here: [https://lnkd.in/ekBjg5yg](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2FekBjg5yg&urlhash=PZnG&trk=public_post-text)

The new consumer investment play: own the lab, not the label
After five months away, I've spent the first few weeks back catching up with friends and clients across consumer and private equity. One theme keeps coming up: investors remain interested in consumer - but increasingly they'd rather own what sits behind the brand than the brand itself.
A few years ago a financial sponsor might have backed a fast-growing haircare or supplements brand, debating influencer metrics at IC. Today, the same investor might prefer the "Contract Development and Manufacturing Organisation" (CDMO) formulating and manufacturing the product - with proprietary R&D, regulatory know-how, and a long list of brands depending on it.
The appeal is straightforward: indirect consumer exposure, sticky B2B revenues, high switching costs - and a lot less guesswork than backing a marketing-led brand.
Recent transactions reflect this:
🧬 [LABOMAR](https://it.linkedin.com/company/labomarspa?trk=public_post-text) – Pan-European nutraceutical CDMO backed by [Charterhouse Capital Partners](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/charterhouse-capital-partners-llp?trk=public_post-text)
💊 [NATURACARE](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/naturacare?trk=public_post-text) and [Sochim International S.R.L](https://it.linkedin.com/company/sochim-international-srl?trk=public_post-text) – Pan-European food supplements platform backed by [DENTRESSANGLE](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/dentressangle?trk=public_post-text)
💄 [Anjac Health & Beauty GROUP](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/anjac-heath-beauty?trk=public_post-text) – beauty and personal care CDMO backed by [KKR](https://www.linkedin.com/company/kkr?trk=public_post-text) and Krefeld
🌿 [P.A. AROMATICS FLAVORS S.r.l.](https://it.linkedin.com/company/p.a.-aromatics-flavors?trk=public_post-text) – fragrance ingredients platform, being acquired by [Investindustrial](https://uk.linkedin.com/company/investindustrial?trk=public_post-text)
None of these are brands consumers recognise on the shelf - but they sit behind dozens of them.
Happy to continue the debate over coffee around Soho or a walk - UK weather permitting 😊


![]()










To view or add a comment, [sign in](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Ftommaso-turri-babb6b88_the-new-consumer-investment-play-own-the-activity-7440411262603264001-lu5X&trk=public_post_feed-cta-banner-cta)

![View profile for Adam Bergman]()

I am in Chicago for The Future of Protein Production Conference, so it’s good timing that [Nick Bradley 🌱🥩](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/nick-bradley-%F0%9F%8C%B1%F0%9F%A5%A9-1a90519?trk=public_post-text) from [The Future of Protein Production](https://nl.linkedin.com/company/the-future-of-protein-production?trk=public_post-text) published an article, "The banker’s reality check, and why it matters in Chicago" based on my conversation with Alex Crisp about the state of the “future protein” sector.
Key takeaways include:
· FoodTech is not like software. Companies do not grow quickly, reach profitability, and achieve an exit in the typical tech 5-7-year time horizon
· Developing future proteins typically involves innovation in biology, which is very difficult. However, unlike the biotechnology sector, even when successful, FoodTech products don’t generate the same huge financial rewards and high margins that companies get when developing a new drug, as the food sector is extremely commoditized with limited opportunity for premium pricing for meat
· The future protein sector is struggling because companies received inflated valuations during the bubble period from 2018 – 2022 by overpromising and creating financial projections that were unrealistic and unachievable
· Capital has become more selective, but is available for companies executing on their business plan with achievable milestones and capital efficient growth strategies
· After strong initial growth, sales at leading plant protein companies, [Beyond Meat](https://www.linkedin.com/company/gobeyondofficial?trk=public_post-text) and [Impossible Foods](https://www.linkedin.com/company/impossible-foods-inc-?trk=public_post-text), have plateaued or gone down. The whole sector has failed to regain momentum, with bankruptcies accelerating
· Future protein areas that seem best poised for success include: ingredients, blended products (animal and future protein), and B2B plays. These are places where future proteins can more easily fit into the existing food supply chain rather than trying to convince consumers to purchase new products that often have an inferior taste and texture, and cost more
